rationale: This page discusses the etymology and historical evolution of the name 'Lucknow', tracing it back to the Hindu epic Ramayana and the 11th-century settlement Lakhanpur.
cleanedText:
The Origins of a Name
Etymology
"Lucknow" is the anglicised spelling of the local pronunciation "Lakhnau". According to legend, the city is named after Lakshmana, a hero of the Hindu epic Ramayana. The legend states that Lakshmana had a palace or estate in the area, called Lakshmanapuri (Lakshmana's city).
Evolution
The settlement came to be known as Lakhanpur (or Lachhmanpur) by the 11th century, and later evolved into Lucknow.
